channel.title: Azure on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Azure on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Network Watcher rule impact analyser
item.description:
Azure Network Watcher rule impact analyzer is now generally available. It lets you assess the potential effect of NSG or security admin rule changes on live network traffic before applying them.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Blob Storage SDK for Rust
item.description:
Azure Blob Storage SDK for Rust is now generally available. It supports connecting to Azure Blob Storage accounts and performing container/blob operations such as upload, download, list, and management.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Container Apps Express
item.description:
Azure Container Apps Express is now in public preview. It is presented as a simplified way to deploy and scale applications on Azure without making infrastructure decisions.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Update: 99.99% uptime for all Azure Service Bus Premium namespaces in Availability Zone regions
item.description:
Starting May 1, 2026, Azure Service Bus Premium namespaces deployed in regions with Availability Zone support will qualify for a 99.99% uptime SLA. This applies to all Premium namespaces in those regions.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Virtual Network Manager rule impact analyzer
item.description:
Azure Virtual Network Manager rule impact analyzer is now generally available. It lets you simulate the effect of security admin rules on virtual networks before deploying them.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Sentinel TI - improved pattern parsing & revoke reliability
item.description:
Sentinel TI is now generally available with two updates: improved pattern parsing accuracy and a fix for revoke actions so they apply reliably. The change is meant to improve control in pattern-based workflows.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in all three available zones in Japan West
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 is now generally available in all three Availability Zones in Japan West. The disk type is positioned as a next-generation general-purpose block storage option for Azure virtual machines with sub-millisecond latency.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Dl/D/E v7 Virtual Machines
item.description:
Azure has made Dl/D/E v7 virtual machines generally available. The new general-purpose and memory-optimized VMs use Intel Xeon 6 (Granite Rapids) processors and claim up to 20% better general compute performance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Document Intelligence v3.0 API will be retired on March 30, 2029
item.description:
Azure Document Intelligence v3.0 API will be retired on March 30, 2029. Users should migrate to the latest generally available v4.0 API before that date.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Reserved Virtual Machines Instances for select VM series
item.description:
Azure will retire certain Reserved VM Instance options for select VM series starting July 1, 2026. One-year purchases and renewals will stop for listed series, and the notice also indicates broader retirement of one-year and three-year reserved instance offerings for those series.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Elastic SAN support for AVS Gen2 Private Cloud
item.description:
Azure Elastic SAN support is now generally available for AVS Gen2 Private Cloud. It simplifies datastore connectivity and configuration by removing the need for an ExpressRoute gateway and using a single Private Endpoint, while also improving performance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Update: Egress and data transfer with Azure in the United Kingdom
item.description:
Azure now offers free egress and at-cost data transfer for customers and CSP partners in the UK when transferring data between Azure and non-Azure environments over the internet. This expands an existing Europe transfer model to the United Kingdom.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Generally Available: Azure Functions support for Java 25
item.description:
Azure Functions now generally supports Java 25. You can develop and deploy Java 25 apps locally to supported Azure Functions plans on Linux and Windows, including Flex Consumption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Memory in Foundry Agent Service
item.description:
Foundry Agent Service now has a preview memory feature for managed long-term memory. It also integrates natively with Microsoft Agent Framework and LangGraph, without requiring external databases to provision, scale, or secure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Public Preview: Container Network Insights Agent
item.description:
Azure has launched a public preview of the Container Network Insights Agent for troubleshooting Kubernetes networking issues. It provides a lightweight, web-based way to inspect and correlate network signals that are otherwise spread across multiple tools.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Microsoft HTTP DDoS Ruleset for Azure WAF on Azure Front Door Premium
item.description:
Microsoft has announced public preview of the HTTP DDoS Ruleset for Azure WAF on Azure Front Door Premium. The feature is intended to help protect against HTTP-layer DDoS attacks, which can bypass traditional static controls.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: High-Performance Distributed Caching with .NET and Postgres on Azure
item.description:
The post introduces a .NET walkthrough for adding distributed caching with Postgres on Azure to improve performance, reduce latency, and lower load on downstream systems. It presents current caching best practices and packages them into a reusable application example.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Hosted Agents in Foundry Agent Service
item.description:
Azure Foundry Agent Service now offers hosted agents in public preview. Each agent session runs in an isolated sandbox with a clean environment, no shared state, and strong compute boundaries.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Foundry Toolkit for Visual Studio Code (formerly AI Toolkit for VS Code)
item.description:
Foundry Toolkit for VS Code is now generally available. It adds VS Code support for Microsoft Agent Framework, with agent creation from templates or GitHub Copilot, local test/debug with visualization and traces, and direct deployment to Foundry.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Capacity Autoscaling
item.description:
Capacity Autoscaling for Elastic SAN is now generally available. It lets customers set a policy to automatically expand SAN capacity based on usage instead of overprovisioning or managing capacity manually.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor pipeline
item.description:
Azure Monitor pipeline is now generally available. It provides a centralized control point for telemetry ingestion and transformation for secure, high-throughput, enterprise-scale scenarios.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Encrypt Premium SSD v2 and Ultra Disks with Cross Tenant Customer Managed Keys
item.description:
Cross-tenant customer-managed keys for Premium SSD v2 and Ultra Disks are now generally available. Managed disks can now be encrypted with a CMK stored in an Azure Key Vault in a different Microsoft Entra tenant.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Pod CIDR expansion in AKS
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) now supports generally available Pod CIDR expansion for overlay-based clusters. This lets teams increase the pod IP range when clusters run out of addresses, avoiding cluster rebuilds.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: AzureDnsEndpoints for Azure storage accounts will be retired March 2027
item.description:
AzureDnsZone endpoints (preview) for Azure Blob storage accounts will be retired in March 2027. Microsoft advises moving all new and existing blob storage account deployments to Standard endpoints.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Service Fabric support for Windows Server 2019 ends on March 31, 2027 - upgrade to Windows Server 2025
item.description:
Service Fabric support for clusters running on Windows Server 2019 ends on March 31, 2027. Customers need to upgrade to Windows Server 2025 by then to stay supported.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: User delegation SAS for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues
item.description:
User delegation SAS is now generally available for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues. It provides a more secure SAS option than account or service SAS by binding the token to the delegator.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in US Gov Arizona
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 is now generally available in US Gov Arizona. The disk type is a next-generation block storage option for Azure VMs and is available in a region without Availability Zones.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: EU data residency region expanding to include EFTA countries
item.description:
Starting May 1, 2026, GitHub Enterprise Cloud on ghe.com will expand the EU data residency region to include Azure infrastructure in EFTA countries, specifically Norway and Switzerland. This affects the EU data residency region for customers using GitHub Enterprise Cloud on ghe.com.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Ephemeral OS Disk with full caching for VM/VMSS
item.description:
Azure VM and VMSS now support Ephemeral OS Disk with full caching in public preview. The OS disk image is cached entirely on local VM storage to improve OS disk performance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift in Indonesia Central
item.description:
Azure Red Hat OpenShift is now generally available in the Indonesia Central Azure region. This expands regional deployment options for customers running OpenShift workloads on Azure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL will retire on March 31, 2029
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL will be retired on March 31, 2029. Users must migrate to Azure Database for PostgreSQL Elastic Cluster before that date.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in South India
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 is now generally available in South India. The region does not have Availability Zones, and the disk type provides sub-millisecond latency as a next-generation general-purpose block storage option for Azure VMs.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Online migration now uses the pgoutput plugin
item.description:
Azure online PostgreSQL migrations now use the pgoutput plugin for generally available online (minimal-downtime) migration. The change is intended to improve reliability, performance, and compatibility with PostgreSQL logical replication.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: AI Agent for container networking troubleshooting
item.description:
Azure has a public preview of an AI Agent for container networking troubleshooting. It provides a lightweight web-based interface to help correlate logs and metrics for Kubernetes networking incidents.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Microsoft Azure Kubernetes Application Network
item.description:
Microsoft Azure Kubernetes Application Network is in public preview. It targets Kubernetes environments spread across regions and clusters by adding application-layer networking controls and visibility beyond IP-based networking.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Sphere will be retired on July 31, 2031
item.description:
Azure Sphere will be retired on July 31, 2031. After that date, Microsoft will stop providing customer application, OS, bug, and security updates, as well as DAA certificate issuance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: HBv2-series Azure Virtual Machines will be retired on May 31, 2027
item.description:
Microsoft Azure will retire HBv2-series VM sizes on May 31, 2027. The affected sizes are Standard_HB120rs_v2, Standard_HB120-96rs_v2, Standard_HB120-64rs_v2, Standard_HB120-32rs_v2, and Standard_HB120-16rs_v2.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: HC-series Azure Virtual Machines will be retired on May 31, 2027
item.description:
Microsoft Azure will retire HC-series VM sizes Standard_HC44rs, Standard_HC44-16rs, and Standard_HC44-32rs on May 31, 2027. Users should plan to migrate to a current supported VM size before the retirement date.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: NP-series Azure Virtual Machines will be retired on May 31, 2027
item.description:
Microsoft Azure will retire NP-series VM sizes Standard_NP10s, Standard_NP20s, and Standard_NP40s on May 31, 2027. Users should migrate workloads to alternative Azure GPU VM sizes before that date to avoid service disruption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Emissions Impact Dashboard for Azure
item.description:
The Emissions Impact Dashboard for Azure, hosted by Power BI, will be retired on March 31, 2027. After that date, it will no longer be accessible and technical support will end.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift Managed Identity and Workload Identity
item.description:
Azure Red Hat OpenShift now generally supports managed identities and workload identities. This lets clusters and applications run on Azure without long-lived service principal credentials.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: SQL MCP Server
item.description:
Azure announced a public preview of SQL MCP Server, an MCP connector for production databases. It is part of Data API Builder and is intended to provide a simple, predictable, and secure way to connect AI agents to data workflows.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Data API builder with built-in GitHub Copilot in MSSQL extension
item.description:
The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code adds Data API builder in public preview with built-in GitHub Copilot integration. It provides a guided experience for generating backend APIs directly in the development workflow.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Lakeflow Connect Free Tier now available in Azure Databricks
item.description:
Azure Databricks has launched a Lakeflow Connect Free Tier in Azure Databricks. Each workspace gets 100 free DBUs per day for ingestion from SaaS applications and databases, which is described as supporting roughly 100 million records per workspace per day.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Foundry Agent Service
item.description:
Microsoft announced general availability of the next-generation Foundry Agent Service. It introduces a redesigned API format and runtime experience for building and operating agents from prototype to production.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: ai: Foundry Agent Service is GA: private networking, Voice Live, and enterprise-grade evaluations
item.description:
Foundry Agent Service is now generally available with several new production features, including end-to-end private networking, Voice Live integration, expanded MCP authentication, GA evaluations with continuous monitoring, and hosted agent deployments in six additional Azure regions.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: azure: Retirement: End of life reminder of NVv3 and NVv4-series Azure virtual machine in Azure Batch pools
item.description:
Microsoft Azure will end support for NVv3 and NVv4-series virtual machines in Azure Batch pools on September 30, 2026. The notice lists affected NVv3 sizes, including Standard_NV12s_v3, NV12hs_v3, NV24s_v3, NV24ms_v3, NV32ms_v3, and NV48s_v3.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure SRE Agent with new capabilities
item.description:
Azure SRE Agent is now generally available. The GA release adds new capabilities focused on incident diagnosis, automated response workflows, and improving uptime while reducing operational toil.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Databricks Lakebase
item.description:
Azure Databricks Lakebase is now generally available as a managed PostgreSQL service designed for OLTP with separated storage and compute.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Databricks update workspace network configuration
item.description:
Azure Databricks now generally supports updating an existing workspace’s network configuration.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk Storage is now available in a third Availability Zone in New Zealan North
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk Storage is now available in a third Availability Zone in the New Zealand North region.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: DCesv6, DCedsv6, ECesv6, and ECedsv6 confidential VMs
item.description:
Azure announced general availability of the DCesv6, DCedsv6, ECesv6, and ECedsv6 confidential VM series based on 5th Gen Intel Xeon processors with Intel Trust Domain Extensions (TDX).

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Managed NGINX Ingress with Application Routing Add-on Retiring November 2026
item.description:
Microsoft will retire the Managed NGINX Ingress used by the AKS Application Routing add-on in November 2026 due to the upstream Ingress-NGINX project deprecating and ending updates after March 2026. Microsoft will provide only critical security patches until 30 Nov 2026, after which the controller is unsupported.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Geo‑redundant backups for Premium SSD v2 in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description:
Azure Database for PostgreSQL now supports configuring geo-redundant automated backups when using Premium SSD v2 disks (public preview). This adds a disaster-recovery option by storing backups in a geo-redundant manner.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Windows Server Annual Channel (preview)
item.description:
Windows Server Annual Channel (Preview) on AKS will retire on May 15, 2026. Users should migrate to Windows Server Long Term Servicing Channel (LTSC) before that date.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: WAF Insights for Application Gateway
item.description:
Azure Application Gateway WAF Insights is now in public preview, adding an interactive view of WAF logs and metrics. It is intended to help investigate blocked requests and analyze attack patterns.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in Brazil Southeast, and in a third Availability Zone in Malaysia West and Indonesia Central
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 disks are now generally available in Brazil Southeast and expanded to a third Availability Zone in Malaysia West and Indonesia Central.

  • Source: Azure Updates
